data.title: AI buzz powers SK Hynix and Samsung stock to record highs
data.description:
• South Korean chipmakers SK Hynix (OTCPK:HXSCF) and Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF) notched record highs on Friday, after a near-weeklong holiday, driven by a series of artificial intelligence deals. 
  • SK Hynix shares jumped 10%, while Samsung Electronics rose nearly 6% to hit fresh records, according to data from FactSet.
  • The two companies are set to benefit from an OpenAI (OPENAI) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) deal that could see Sam Altman’s company take a 10% stake in AMD.
  • Shares of AMD rallied on the news and are up more than 40% so far this week.
  • Separately, Samsung's (OTCPK:SSNLF) 12-layer, third-generation high-bandwidth memory chips, or HBM3E, have reportedly received approval for usage by Nvidia (NVDA).

data.title: Coinbase and Mastercard in talks to acquire stablecoin fintech BVNK - report
data.description:
Crypto exchange Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) and payments giant Mastercard (NYSE:MA) have held advanced discussions to acquire BVNK, a London-based fintech focused on stablecoin payment infrastructure, Fortune reported, citing six people familiar with the matter.

While the talks are not yet finalized, several sources told Fortune that the potential deal could value BVNK between $1.5 billion and $2.5 billion. Three of those sources said Coinbase currently appears to be ahead of Mastercard in the bidding process.

If completed, the acquisition would mark the largest-ever deal involving stablecoin infrastructure, underscoring growing competition between traditional financial firms and crypto players to shape the future of digital payments.

BVNK provides enterprise-grade infrastructure for stablecoin payments. The company said Thursday that it has received an investment from Citi (C), but did not disclose the size of the investment or its current valuation. Co-founder Chris Harmse told CNBC that BVNK is now valued above the $750 million reported at its last funding round.

data.title: Vortex Metals to close second tranche of private placement for $105K
data.description:
• Vortex Metals (TSXV:VMS:CA) (OTCQB:VMSSF) plans to close a second tranche of its private placement due to demand from an existing strategic investor.
  • The company will issue up to 2.3M units at $0.04 per unit, for total gross proceeds of $105,000.
  • Each unit includes one common share and half of a warrant; each full warrant is exercisable at $0.08 for 36 months.
  • Use of proceeds: 40% for mining concession fees, 40% for exploration, and 20% for working capital.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Nurix Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$1.03 misses by $0.11, revenue of $7.9M misses by $4.33M
data.description:
• Nurix Therapeutics press release (NASDAQ:NRIX): Q3 GAAP EPS of -$1.03 misses by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $7.9M (-37.3% Y/Y) misses by $4.33M.
  • Cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities were $428.8 million as of August 31, 2025, compared to $609.6 million as of November 30, 2024.

data.title: Bollinger Innovations to move stock listing from Nasdaq to OTC Markets
data.description:
• Bollinger Innovations (NASDAQ:BINI) will transition its stock listing to the OTCID market, part of the broader OTC Markets, effective October 13, 2025.
  • The company will retain its ticker symbol "BINI" and continue to meet SEC reporting requirements.
  • The move comes after non-compliance with Nasdaq, which requires a minimum market value of $35M.
  • Bollinger voluntarily withdrew from the Nasdaq hearings process and opted for a cost-effective, flexible listing on OTC Markets.
  • Bollinger aims to reduce regulatory costs and redirect resources toward expanding its commercial electric vehicle business. The company is considering upgrades within OTC Markets tiers and may also seek an international listing, including on the London Stock Exchange’s AIM.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Gunnison Copper appoints Robert Winton as COO
Asia markets down; U.S. shutdown and Japan inflation weigh on sentiment
data.description:
Asia stocks trade lower on Friday, tracking weakness on Wall Street after funding bills aimed at ending the US government shutdown failed to pass the Senate for the seventh time on Thursday. Traders assessed remarks from Fed officials on interest rates and the start of the U.S. earnings season. 

The U.S. on Thursday imposed sanctions on about 100 individuals, entities, and vessels accused of aiding Iran’s oil and petrochemical trade, the Trump administration said.

Silver edged down to around $49 per ounce on Friday but remained on track for its eighth consecutive weekly advance. Gold rose to around $3,980 per ounce on Friday, heading for its eighth consecutive weekly gain, after notching a record high earlier in the week.

Japan (NKY:IND) fell 0.84% to below 48,400, while the broader Topix dropped 1.1% to 3,220 on Friday. The Japanese yen traded at an eight-month low around 153 per dollar on Friday and was on track to decline nearly 4% for the week, marking its steepest decline since September last year.

Japan’s producer prices rose 2.7% year-on-year in September 2025, keeping the same pace as in the previous month but exceeding market forecasts of 2.5%. 

Japan’s bank lending rose 3.8% in September 2025, accelerating from a downwardly revised 3.5% gain in August and exceeding market expectations for a 3.7% growth.

Japan’s ruling party chief Sanae Takaichi said Thursday that if elected prime minister, she would immediately push for measures to ease the economic impact of rising living costs. 

China (SHCOMP) fell 0.58% to around 3,920, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 0.8% to 13,613 on Friday, giving back part of the prior session’s gains amid soft global cues. The offshore yuan  

Investors now turn their attention to the Communist Party’s leadership meeting on Oct. 20–23 and a possible Xi-Trump meeting at the APEC summit later this month for new policy signals. 

Caution also grew ahead of key Chinese data next week, including September trade figures, CPI and PPI readings, and credit statistics.

President Donald Trump predicted that China would soon resume purchases of U.S. soybeans following his upcoming meeting with President Xi Jinping, despite ongoing trade tensions.

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 1.38% to 26,501 in early trade on Friday, marking a fifth straight fall and the lowest in near two weeks.

India (SENSEX) rose 0.45% to 82,507 in morning trade on Friday, gaining for the second straight session.

Australia (AS51) fell 0.13% to around 8,955 in Friday morning trade, reversing the previous session’s gains, as gold and energy stocks dragged the market lower amid easing commodity prices. The Australian dollar appreciated to around $0.657 on Friday, slightly rebounding from a nearly two-week low touched the previous day after RBA Governor Michele Bullock struck cautious remarks.

Australia’s economy is performing well, with inflation within the central bank's 2–3% target range and a tight labor market, Governor Michele Bullock told a parliamentary committee Friday. 

In the U.S. on Thursday, all three major indexes ended lower as investors paused to digest optimism around AI, interest-rate cuts, and the ongoing government shutdown. 

U.S. stock futures inched up on Friday after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite retreated from record highs in the prior session: Dow +0.14%; S&P 500 +0.13%; Nasdaq +0.17%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

Japan's producer prices rises more than expected in September to 2.7%

U.S. sanctions 29 companies in China, Turkey, and UAE over Iran military support

China tightens export rules for crucial rare earths and related technology

Japan services sector expands for sixth month, but jobless rate hits 13-month high

Australia’s services sector expands for 20th straight month despite sharp slowdown in September

data.title: SoftBank in talks for $5B margin loan backed by Arm shares to fund OpenAI investment - report
data.description:
• SoftBank Group (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) is in talks to borrow $5 billion from global banks for a margin loan secured by shares of its chip unit Arm Holdings, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.
  • The capital will fund additional investment in Microsoft-backed OpenAI (OPENAI) this year, the report added, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • A margin loan is a type of loan where you borrow money using your investments, such as stocks, as collateral.
  • SoftBank has raised a total of $13.5 billion in margin loans from Arm shares, with $5 billion still undrawn as of March 2025, and the latest facility will increase the total to $18.5 billion, the report added.

data.title: Toogood Gold launches C$2M private placement to fund exploration in Newfoundland
data.description:
• Toogood Gold Corp. (TSXV:TGC:CA) has announced a non-brokered private placement to raise up to C$2M.
  • The offering will consist of 6.67M units priced at C$0.30 per unit.
  • Each unit includes one common share and half of a common share purchase warrant.
  • Each full warrant is exercisable at C$0.45 for a period of two years.
  • Proceeds will fund working capital and exploration at the Toogood Gold Project on New World Island, Newfoundland.
  • The offering is expected to close on or around October 22, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Apple leadership shuffle: Eddy Cue to lead health, Craig Federighi takes watchOS
data.description:
• Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is reportedly preparing to expand the roles of some top executives in response to the pending departure of longtime Chief Operating Officer Jeff Williams.
  • Services chief Eddy Cue will gain supervision over Apple’s (NASDAQ:AAPL) health and fitness teams, while Craig Federighi, head of software engineering, will now oversee the Apple Watch operating system, Bloomberg News reported.
  • This move comes as the iPhone maker is preparing to launch its new Health+ subscription service in 2026, signaling a growing focus on wellness as part of its services business.
  • Williams, who has steered operations since 2015, has already handed off his portfolio—including supply chain, AppleCare, and China operations—to Sabih Khan, the company’s new operating chief.
